what_it_does: This file is a ground-truth inventory of the installed episodic-memory plugin (v1.0.15) and its upstream counterpart (v1.4.2), documenting observed file paths, data sizes, source-code structure, runtime behavior, adaptation seams, and related system references from direct reads and live database queries.
observations: Installed version is 1.0.15 at a cached plugin path; upstream clone is v1.4.2.
The live database is 10.87 GB SQLite with 75,374 exchanges, 75,375 vectors, and 6,713,952 tool_calls rows.
The archive holds 13 GB across 29,678 JSONL files, but only 13,270 are indexed and 1,267 have summaries.
Embedding model is hardcoded to Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2, producing 384-dimensional vectors locally.
A SessionStart hook runs sync --background on every Claude Code startup/resume.
Sync only indexes files copied in the current run, leaving a large pre-existing archive backlog.
Text search uses SQL LIKE rather than FTS5, contrary to the shipped MCP-TOOLS.md reference.
The MCP read tool has no path sandboxing and can read any absolute JSONL file.
The shipped agent manifest lists a show tool, but the MCP server registers read.
Tool results are never captured into the database despite 6.7M tool_calls rows.
A parallel SessionEnd pipeline uploads the same source JSONLs to Supabase.
A custom lobe-importer.py has already written non-Claude conversations into the archive format.
Upstream v1.4.2 adds file locking, embedding migration, summarizer reentrancy guard, and summary error sentinels.
standouts: It is an adaptation-oriented forensic inventory, not a user or operational guide.
The local data footprint is large while the indexed subset lags far behind the archive.
The embedding provider is the central adaptation seam: one hardcoded module plus a duplicated dimension.
Collection continues via the SessionStart hook even though a user directive forbids recall use.
Upstream v1.4.2 contains concurrency and migration hardening directly relevant to this machine.
conventions: Labels every claim as Observed or Inferred to grade evidentiary strength.
Uses numbered sections, tables, and code-line citations for technical inventory.
Distinguishes installed version from upstream version and maps file-by-file responsibilities.
Treats environment variables as the primary configuration seam.
Mixes live database metrics with source-code reading in a single document.
concerns: The inventory mixes observed facts and inferred behavior, occasionally without inline evidence.
Some claims about upstream fixes and runtime behavior are labeled Inferred rather than execution-verified.
It interprets a user directive ('Don't use episodic memory') and strategic purpose, slightly blurring neutral inventory with advocacy.
Heavy use of absolute, single-machine paths limits portability of the document itself.
No symbol list is provided despite the file's code-level detail.
notes: A dense, evidence-typed inventory of the episodic-memory plugin's installed state, source seams, and data footprint, intended to support a Company fork rather than continued use.
questions: What prompted the no-episodic-memory directive and how does it affect the still-running collection hook?
Is the 10-per-run summary cap configurable in v1.0.15 or hardcoded?
How will the Company reconcile this archive with the parallel Supabase conversation pipeline?
Has upstream v1.4.2 been validated against this machine's 10 GB database?
Who maintains lobe-importer.py and is it still in active use?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is an inventory of what exists for adaptation; the file does that, while also embedding adaptation recommendations and a usage directive, broadening the inventory role slightly.
apparent_themes: Separation of collection from recall consumption
intention_signals: It is trying to give a future maintainer a complete, evidence-graded snapshot of a complex plugin installation so the useful mechanics can be harvested for a Company-specific substrate while respecting a user ban on direct recall use.
novelty: Unusually detailed forensic inventory of a third-party plugin at the code-line and live-database level, explicitly framed as mining material for a replacement system rather than as operational documentation.
contribution: It contributes a pre-digested map of hardcoded seams, data volumes, source-code responsibilities, configuration env vars, and operational gaps that a fused system can use to decide what to port, replace, or discard.
summary_for_embedding: INVENTORY.md is a ground-truth, observation-graded inventory of the installed episodic-memory plugin v1.0.15, its 10.87 GB SQLite index and 13 GB conversation archive, source-code structure, runtime hook behavior, adaptation seams, upstream v1.4.2 deltas, and related system references.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to enable a Company fork by documenting exactly what exists, where the hardcoded coupling points are, and what operational hardening should be inherited from upstream.
